title: campanula carnica subsp. consanguinea
intro: Campanula carnica subsp. consanguinea, also known as the Carpathian bellflower, is a subspecies of the Campanula carnica plant, a type of flowering plant in the bellflower family. It is primarily used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems, due to its rich content of essential oils and phenolic compounds. Campanula carnica subsp. consanguinea is traditionally prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of homeopathic remedies.
